vix.note: VIX up 6% to 21.06 as AI-driven tech selloff deepens; AVGO guidance miss and hawkish Fed repricing combine to lift volatility. Previous session settled at 19.87.

macro: AI selloff deepens as AVGO guidance miss triggers $1.4T sector wipeout. Nasdaq fell 4%+ on June 5 after Broadcom's Q3 AI chip sales forecast disappointed, erasing roughly $1.4T in semiconductor market cap. Strong jobs data pushed Fed cut expectations to early 2027 at best, with Chair Warsh holding rates firm and Treasury yields at multi-year highs. VIX up 6% to 21.06 today. Nasdaq recovered 0.9% on June 8 as analysts called the selloff positioning-led -- OpenAI and Apple both expanding GPU-backed infrastructure signals underlying AI capex demand remains intact.
action: Hold existing positions; wait for SPY to reclaim 745 or NVDA to base above 210 before adding risk -- VIX at 21 and Fed-hold headwinds favor patience over aggression today.

long.h: OpenAI weighs leasing Ohio data center with Nvidia backing
long.p: OpenAI is in negotiations to lease a proposed 10-gigawatt data center campus on federal land in Ohio, potentially backed by Nvidia -- signaling continued GPU demand despite near-term market volatility.

long.h: Apple to run advanced AI model on Nvidia GPUs via Google Cloud
long.p: Apple is outsourcing its most advanced AI workloads to Nvidia GPUs on Google Cloud, a notable shift away from private infrastructure and a strong endorsement of NVDA hardware dominance in frontier AI.

long.h: Broadcom falls despite new AI data center deal with Apollo and Blackstone
long.p: AVGO shares continued lower despite a major new AI infrastructure partnership with Apollo Global and Blackstone, highlighting how far the selloff has overtaken positive near-term news flow for the sector.

long.h: Intel up 168% in 2026 as analysts forecast 159% earnings growth
long.p: Intel's 2026 rally is grounded in real operational improvement: rising Xeon CPU demand from AI data centers and a credible path to restored profitability. Analysts debate whether the stock is a bargain or a trap at current levels.

long.h: AI trade worst stretch in months -- three reasons the selloff could be a buying opportunity
long.p: The AI selloff is driven by positioning and guidance disappointment, not a collapse in underlying capex. Three fundamental signals suggest the dip may be worth buying for investors with a 6-to-12-month horizon.

short.h: Nasdaq worst day of year as AI stocks tumble and Fed rate-hike odds rise
short.p: Strong May jobs data triggered a hawkish Fed repricing and a 4%+ Nasdaq decline. AI and semiconductor stocks led the drop as investors priced out near-term rate cuts.

short.h: Broadcom Q3 AI chip guidance miss triggers $1.4T semiconductor wipeout
short.p: Broadcom's failure to raise its full-year AI chip forecast sent shares down 14% and erased an estimated $1.4T across the sector on June 5, tightening risk sentiment across the entire AI supply chain.

short.h: Wells Fargo warns investors after AI selloff -- slower rally ahead
short.p: Wells Fargo analysts called the AI selloff a wake-up call, warning investors to expect a slower pace of gains even if AI fundamentals remain intact. The move does not signal a structural breakdown.

short.h: Fed Chair Warsh holds rates firm -- no cuts expected before 2027
short.p: The Federal Reserve is expected to hold rates through all of 2026, with cuts possible only in early 2027 if inflation decelerates. Treasury yields at multi-year highs continue to compress the equity risk premium.

short.h: Semiconductor selloff read as technical correction -- Nasdaq recovers 0.9% on June 8
short.p: Nasdaq regained 0.9% by June 8 as analysts flagged the selloff as positioning-driven, not fundamental. Underlying AI capex demand appears intact with no reversal in hyperscaler spending plans.
